01CONFIDENTIAL

alcheTHE INSIGHT / FEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

"People who want to age well are forcedto become full-time project managers oftheir own health."

In an environment of infinite wellness options, trusted curation becomes more valuable than

product proliferation. Longevity is no longer inaccessible. It is overwhelming. Consumers are not

struggling to find options. They are struggling to decide what is worth trusting, combining, and

repeating.

The problem is not lack of supply.

It is lack of intelligent, taste-driven curation developed in consultation with medical professionals.

[01] THE INS I G H T

02CONFIDENTIAL

alcheTHE INSIGHT / FEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[01] THE INS I G H TWhat is broken about how peopleengage with longevity?

01Discovery

Hears about longevity from a podcast, book, or friend. Excitement peaks.

02Research

Finds 300,000+ supplement SKUs, contradictory protocols, influencer advice. Overwhelm begins.

Buys from 4 to 6 different brands. Downloads 2 to 3 apps. No connection between them.03

Scattered Purchases

04Self-Curation

Attempts to build their own stack. Spends EUR 150 to 400/mo across fragmented services.

05Decision Fatigue

Cannot evaluate quality, dosage, interactions, or timing. Guilt replaces excitement.

06Abandonment

Gives up within 60 days. Returns to old habits. 96% digital health app churn rate.

66

66 days to form a health habit. 96% of users churn before they get there. The window betweeninitial motivation and lasting behavior change is where nearly every wellness product fails.

DAYS

Lally et al. (2010), European Journal of Social Psychology; Baumel et al. (2019), Journal of Medical Internet Research; RevenueCat State ofSubscription Apps (2025).

03CONFIDENTIAL

alcheTHE INSIGHT / FEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[ 0 1 ] T H E I N S I G H TWhy now?

Three forces have converged to create a once-in-a-decade entry point.

Force 1: Demand exploded. Infrastructure did not.

Peter Attia's Outlive: #1 NYT bestseller, sold 3M+ copies globally

Andrew Huberman: 10M+ followers across platforms

Bryan Johnson: made anti-aging cultural, mainstream media coverage worldwide

Euromonitor 2025: healthspan ranked #1 global consumer trend

84% of consumers now rank wellness as a top priority (GWI 2024)

"Consumer awareness has outpaced every product category built to serve it."

Force 2: Germany's prevention gap is an open market.

EUR 474B spent on healthcare annually (12.3% of GDP)

Only 3.1% allocated to prevention

7.8 hospital beds per 1,000 population (OECD average: 4.2)

62% of Germans say the system focuses too much on treatment

"The gap is not a policy debate. It is an open market."

Force 3: Technology is ready. No company has integrated it.

CGM sensors: real-time metabolic tracking, consumer-grade

AI: personalized protocol generation at scale

Wearables: sleep, HRV, stress monitoring (validated accuracy)

Red light therapy: clinically validated, growing adoption

Functional nutrition: proven category, fragmented delivery

"No company has integrated a physical space, a proprietary app, a scalable product brand, curated retail, and cultural event programminginto one data-enabled business model."

04CONFIDENTIAL

alcheTHE INSIGHT / FEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[ 0 1 ] T H E I N S I G H TWho is left out?

UNSERVED

The Missing Consumer

Fewer options, better selected

Integrated rituals, not scattered products

Authority without medical intimidation

Taste without pseudoscience

Has purchasing power, aesthetic sensitivity,high LTV

"No ecosystem built for them."

SHARED CHARACTERISTICS ACROSS ALL SEGMENTS

Value experience over products. Willing to pay premium for quality. Already spend on fragmented wellness. Digital-native,expect app integration. Respond to community and culture.

Is this a vitamin or a painkiller?

This is a painkiller.

alche is a painkiller for a population hemorrhaging time, money, and motivation on wellness fragmentation. The

pain is measurable. The market is quantifiable. The timing window is 18 to 24 months before US players localize

to DACH.

Sources: Euromonitor International (2025); OECD Health at a Glance (2023); Destatis (2023); RevenueCat (2025); Lally et al. (2010); Baumel et

al. (2019); Global Wellness Institute (2024).

WHO WE SERVEFEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[02] WHO WE S E R V ELongevity for people who actually live.

Not the 1% who cold-plunge at 5 AM. The millions who care about aging well but havebeen given fragmented tools that deliver data without direction.

PRIMARY PERSONA

Mira

34 / Berlin / Product Designer / EUR 72,000

Runs three times a week. Takes magnesium and vitamin D but is not confident about dosing. Wore an Oura ring for five

months, checked her sleep score every morning until it started giving her anxiety about not sleeping well enough. The ring

is on her nightstand.

Tried Levels for two months. Learned bananas spike her glucose. Thought "...now what?" and cancelled. Has a supplement

shelf with six half-empty bottles. She does not want to optimize. She wants to live fully and age well without it becoming

her second job.

WHY BERLIN

The ecosystem is forming here.

Berlin has the highest concentration of health-conscious expats in DACH, a 15-20% English-speaking

population matching alche's content language, and lower operational costs that extend runway. The

Biohackers Berlin Meetup alone has 2,213 members.

ENGLISH-SPEAKING

POPULATION

MEETUP MEMBERS

MARKET VALIDATION

27% of Germans are willing to pay out-of-pocket for health apps (SpringerMedizin). Berlin tech worker median salary: EUR

75,000 gross (GfK). The purchasing power and willingness to pay for curated health experiences are already proven in this

market.

alche

THE MARKETFEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[03] THE MAR K E TThe European longevity market isforming now.

No integrated player exists. That changes within 12-18 months.

THE GERMAN HEALTH PARADOX

Germany spends nearly EUR 495 billion on healthcare annually (12.3% of GDP), the highest in the EU. Only 4.8% goes to

prevention. With 7.7 hospital beds per 1,000 people (OECD average: 4.2), the system was architecturally built to treat illness, not

prevent it. Consumers are filling the gap themselves.

COUNTRYHEALTH SPEND (% GDP)LIFE EXPECTANCYGAP

Switzerland11.3%83.4 yrs+2.3 yrs, spends less

France12.1%82.2 yrs+1.1 yrs, spends less

+3.4 yrs, spends less

Japan11.5%84.5yrs

OECD avg9.3%80.3 yrs

OECD Health at a Glance 2025 / Destatis 2023 / GWI 2024 Global Wellness Economy Monitor

12-18 months before well-funded incumbents integrate Europeanlongevity protocols.

THE DATA FEE D B A C K L O O P

The entire system is built around a feedback loop that connects physical and digital layers. This is the core

contribution: a continuous data-driven feedback loop.

  1. AI Glow Scan + Goal
  2. Personalised Protocol

2A. Doctor validates and

Selection

→→→→

Created

enhances protocol (CGM

users)

  1. Booking (LED +smoothie pre-order)
  2. Experience (physical

visit)

→→→→

5A. CGM Glucose Stream

  1. Insights Generated
  2. Recommendations(replenishment, events,

(continuous biometric

data)

(patterns + glucose

correlations)

protocol adjust)

7A. Doctor Review

(medical-gradeadjustment based on CGM

→

  1. User Re-Entry(smarter protocol,continuous learning)

+ all data)

The doctor does not slow down the loop. Steps 2A and 7A operate asynchronously. The user continues through the loop without interruption while the

doctor reviews data and refines the protocol in the background. When an update is ready, the user receives a notification: "Dr. Jarmoukli has updated

your protocol. View your new wellness plan." This architecture transforms alche from a simple wellness app into a medical-grade wellness intelligence

platform.

INTELLIGENCE L A Y E R P R E V I E W

PHYSICAL SPACE = PHASE 2

The Berlin flagship in Mitte is where digital meets physical: LED sessions, functional smoothies, curated products, community

events. It operates near breakeven standalone, generates EUR 8,800 to 11,700/month in subscriber LTV through walk-in

conversions, and creates the environmental cues that bridge the 66-day habit formation gap.

GO-TO-MARKETFEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[08] GO-TO-M A R K E TBuild the audience before the product.

Content-first. Community-first. The product comes to people who already trust thevoice.

Most startups spend money to find customers. We find customers for free, then spend money to build

what they already want. Our content-first strategy trades founder labor for capital: every video,

newsletter, and community event builds an audience that converts directly into our waitlist, then our

founding members, then our recurring revenue.

TWO FLYWHEEL S , S E Q U E N C E D

FLYWHEEL 1 , R U N N I N G N O W

F L Y W H E E L 2 , A C T I V A T E D B Y F U N D I N G

The Content Engine

The Product Engine

Create radical-honesty science content → attract health-

Know your body (data) → Do the protocols (action) → Get what

curious audience → they engage and share → we learn what

you need (products) → Belong with others (community).

they need → create sharper content. No product needed. No

Funding builds the product for an audience that already exists

funding needed.

and trusts us.

CHANNEL STRA T E G Y

PRIMARY: SHORT-FORM VIDEO

AMPLIFIER: YOUTUBE

10-20 minute deep research episodes. Evergreen SEO pillars that

breaking format: first piece is a collagen deep dive across 23 studies

rank in search for years, the moat no short-form competitor builds.

showing only industry-funded research is positive. This IS the brand

voice.

AMPLIFIER: BERLIN EVENTS

SECONDARY: NEWSLETTER

Weekly email, Substack or Beehiiv

Science talks over wine at existing Berlin venues. One evening

generates a month of content. Doubles as customer discovery.

One deep dive per week. Captures emails, the only owned asset.

Biohackers Berlin meetup (2,213 members) is the warm audience.

Social algorithms change; email lists compound. Where the most

engaged followers live and conversion happens.

E X E C U T I O N R O A D M A P12 months to product-market fit.

Foundation

PRE-FUNDING // EUR 0 BUDGET // CONTENT ENGINE IGNITION

Q1M1-3

Launch @alche.space. 3-5 short-form videos per week, honest science format.

Launch newsletter (Week 2). Goal: 300 subscribers by end of Q1.

10 informal customer discovery conversations from social DMs and meetup contacts.

Traction Proof

Q2

PRE-FUNDING // EUR 200-500 EVENTS // WAITLIST OPENS

M4-6

First Alche Salon event. 30-40 people. Film everything, one event produces 3-4 weeks of content.

Open waitlist landing page. Goal: 500 signups from newsletter + social + events.

Begin GmbH formation. Apply to GruendungsBONUS Plus (EUR 50K non-dilutive Berlin grant).

Fundraise + Build

FUNDING CLOSES // EUR 500K RAISE // MVP DEVELOPMENT BEGINS

Q3

M7-9

Pitch with real numbers: followers, newsletter subs, waitlist, event attendees.

Post-funding: hire technical co-founder / dev lead. Begin MVP development.

Events scale to monthly with 60-80 attendees.

Beta Launch

POST-FUNDING // PRODUCT IN HANDS // FOUNDING MEMBERS

Q4

M10-12

MVP beta to first 50-100 users from waitlist. Founding member pricing: EUR 19/month, locked for life.

Content evolves to include product teasers, beta user stories, "building in public" updates.

Physical space search begins. Berlin location planning for hybrid model proof.

alche

TRACTIONFEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[09] TRACTIO N9 conversations that changed ourproduct.3 hypotheses we killed.

No product yet. What we bring: proof of market understanding, intellectual honesty,and a 90-day plan to generate the data that matters.

CUSTOMER DIS C O V E R Y , 9 I N T E R V I E W S

Health-conscious professionals in Berlin, 28-45, 60-min open-ended sessions.

01

"Longevity with intention" tested strongest. Interviewees rejected both "biohacker" and "anti-perfectionist" labels.

02

Fragmentation creates guilt, not just inconvenience. People feel like they're failing at wellness because the ecosystem is impossible to navigate.

03

Accountability without judgment. Small consistent groups over leaderboards. Gamification does not work for longevity.

04

Already spending EUR 50-200/mo on fragmented services , willing to consolidate. Non-spenders need a trigger event (content-first GTM).

05

Local community events as entry point got the strongest positive signal of anything tested.

WHAT CHANGED BASED ON INTERVIEWS

Positioning: "longevity with intention" replaced "anti-perfectionist." Community: small accountability circles, not open forums.

Entry point: content + local events first, app second.

1 Alche competitive spend analysis, Feb 2026 / 2 Reddit subscriber counts, Feb 2026 / 3 Competitive intelligence; no US player has announced

W H A T F U N D I N G S O L V E S

Market demand (711K+ community, $8.9-21K/yrfragmented spend)

HYPOTHESES I N V A L I D A T E D

"Anti-perfectionist" is the right language

Killed by interviews. Reactive, confusing. "Longevity with intention" emerged from users' own words.

Physical space first, digital second

Killed by Berlin failure patterns. Capital-intensive physical retail fails. Model flipped: digital-first.

Bootstrap as a lifestyle business

Killed by competitive timing. 18-24 month window. Speed to market matters more than capital efficiency.

90-DAY TRACT I O N R O A D M A P

DAY 1-30

Foundation

GmbH formed. 20 interviews complete. Waitlist live. CTO search launched.

DAY 30-60

Traction

Content channel at 1,000+ followers. First Berlin event (50+ attendees). Medical advisor formalized.

DAY 60-90

Momentum

MVP spec finalized, development underway. Community pilot with founding members. Measurable KPIs: waitlist conversion,

event attendance, content engagement.

WHAT AI CANN O T R E P L A C E

If Apple Health + ChatGPT gives personalized longevity advice for free, the pure data-and-insights

layer is commoditized. What survives: physical experiences (AI cannot give you a smoothie or a

community event), social identity and belonging (people join for community, not because the

algorithm is optimal), accountability and motivation (knowing what to do is not the hard part, doing it

consistently is).

INTEGRATION IS THE THESIS

None of alche's competitors combine biomarker data + personalized protocols + curated products + physical experiences +

community in one platform. They each do one or two layers. Integration is structurally hard to bolt on because it requires

different organizational DNA than single-vertical excellence. GDPR-native data trust gives a European entrant a structural

advantage US platforms cannot retrofit. When the data layer gets cheaper and AI interpretation improves, the companies that

built genuine community, cultural identity, and regulatory trust are the ones still standing.

Competitive analysis: 30+ companies, Feb 2026 / Blue Zones / WHOOP 2025 Retention Data / Fortune Business Insights 2025

CLOSINGFEBRUARY 2026 // CONFIDENTIAL

[13] CLOSINGThe ambition.

Berlin is the proof. Europe is the unlock.

T H E E N D G A M EThe longevity lifestyle platform

for all of Europe.

What Aesop did for skincare, took something technical and made it beautiful, accessible,

and identity-forming. That is what alche does for longevity.

FROM HERE TO T H E R E

PHASE 1 // M0-12Berlin proof of concept

App launch. One physical space in Mitte. 145 paying subscribers. Break-even on unit economics. Content flywheel spinning.

Community density forming.

PHASE 2 // M6-18Digital expansion across EU

Open app beyond Berlin. Ship products across the EU. Partner with existing wellness spaces. Prove digital-only members

retain without a physical node.

PHASE 3 // M12-

Second location + seed round

24

Second Berlin location where digital density is highest. Pop-ups before permanent. Seed round at EUR 2M+ MRR trajectory.

PHASE 4 // M18-

Platform maturity

36

Open API for partner integrations. Corporate wellness tier. Expand category presence across EU.

What alche is not

What alche is

Not a wellness brand that happens to have an app. Not a

The digital platform is the business. The physical space is one

longevity clinic for the already-converted. Not another

revenue module. Built in Berlin for European culture, regulation,

personalized nutrition play. Not a Silicon Valley export.

and sensibility. GDPR-native from day one. An identity brand,

not a feature set.
